title: Registers and Basic Instructions
summary: Use ARM registers and write basic MOV, ADD, SUB, and data processing instructions.
content_html:
Registers

Registers are small, extremely fast storage locations inside the CPU. ARM has 16 general-purpose 32-bit registers named R0 through R15. Most programs use R0-R12 for general computation.

Register  Purpose
R0-R12    General purpose (R0 often holds function return value)
R13 (SP)  Stack Pointer - points to top of the stack
R14 (LR)  Link Register - holds return address for functions
R15 (PC)  Program Counter - address of next instruction

The MOV Instruction

MOV copies a value into a register. The source can be an immediate constant or another register.

MOV R0, #5        ; R0 = 5 (immediate value)
MOV R1, #0xFF    ; R1 = 255
MOV R2, R0       ; R2 = R0 (copy register)
MOV R3, #0       ; R3 = 0 (clear register)

Data Processing Instructions

ARM data processing instructions follow the pattern:
INSTRUCTION Rd, Rn, Operand2
Where Rd = destination, Rn = source register, Operand2 = register or immediate.

Arithmetic

ADD R0, R1, R2     ; R0 = R1 + R2
ADD R0, R0, #1    ; R0 = R0 + 1  (increment)
SUB R0, R1, R2    ; R0 = R1 - R2
SUB R0, R0, #5    ; R0 = R0 - 5
MUL R0, R1, R2    ; R0 = R1 * R2  (no immediate)

Logical

AND R0, R1, R2     ; R0 = R1 AND R2 (bitwise)
ORR R0, R1, R2     ; R0 = R1 OR  R2 (bitwise)
EOR R0, R1, R2     ; R0 = R1 XOR R2 (bitwise)
MVN R0, R1         ; R0 = NOT R1  (bitwise complement)

Shift Operations

MOV R0, R1, LSL #2   ; R0 = R1 shifted left 2 bits (x4)
MOV R0, R1, LSR #1   ; R0 = R1 shifted right 1 bit (/2)
MOV R0, R1, ASR #1   ; Arithmetic shift right (preserves sign)

Immediate Values

In ARM assembly, immediate values are prefixed with #. However, not all values can be immediate - only values that fit in 8 bits after rotation. Use labels and LDR for large constants.

MOV R0, #255     ; OK - fits in 8 bits
MOV R0, #256     ; OK - 256 = 1 shifted left 8
MOV R0, #300     ; May not be valid as immediate - use LDR

CPSR - Status Flags

The Current Program Status Register holds flags set after arithmetic operations (when using the 'S' suffix):

ADDS R0, R1, R2  ; Performs ADD and sets flags
Flags: N (negative), Z (zero), C (carry), V (overflow)

Program Structure

; Minimal ARM bare metal program (FASMARM)
include 'LIB/FASMARM.INC'

_start:
  MOV R0, #5
  MOV R1, #3
  ADD R2, R0, R1    ; R2 = 8
  HALT              ; Stop execution
